details.finding: Session processor has two-layer architecture: OUTER loop (prompt.ts) handles agent switching/compaction, INNER loop (processor.ts) is pure LLM streaming. Agent switching only happens at outer loop boundary — the inner loop is agent-agnostic. Adding per-agent lifecycle state machines belongs in the outer loop, not the processor.
details.note: Confirmed by cartographer-B analysis of runLoop in prompt.ts:1244 and process() in processor.ts:756

details.finding: All 7 .sql.ts files have complete .pg.sql.ts equivalents. The 3% on roadmap is for application-layer migration (import switching + dual-write), not schema definitions.
details.note: Lane-2 completed handoff. Schema layer is done.
